title: GitButler's Grit experiment rewrites Git in Rust with agents
friendlyTitle: GitButler tested rebuilding Git with help from agents
summary: Scott Chacon described Grit, a library-first Rust reimplementation of Git built with extensive agent assistance. The project reportedly passes 99.3% of Git's C test suite, though the author warns it is not production-ready and may corrupt data.
friendlySummary: GitButler described Grit, a Rust rewrite of Git built with heavy agent help. It passes many tests, but it is not safe for production yet.
whyItMatters: The story is a grounded example of what agents can and cannot do in large software rewrites: they can generate enormous progress against tests, but human judgment and real-world validation still matter. For developers, it points toward a future where agents help modernize critical infrastructure without making review optional.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows agents can speed up large software rewrites, but human review and real testing are still essential.
